questions.question: What should the 4th argument of VLOOKUP almost always be?
questions.explanation: FALSE forces an exact match. TRUE (or omitting it) does an approximate match that silently returns wrong answers.

questions.question: VLOOKUP matches your lookup value against which part of the table?
questions.explanation: VLOOKUP searches only the first column and returns a value from a column to its right — it can't look left.

questions.question: To bring each brand's manager onto the Sales table, which formula is right?
questions.explanation: Look up the Brand in the Brands table and return column 3 (BrandManager). The Reps table is for sales reps, not brands.

questions.question: Two cells look identical but VLOOKUP returns #N/A. Most common cause?
options: One value is a number stored as text, or has trailing spaces
questions.explanation: Type mismatches (text vs number) and invisible spaces are the classic #N/A causes. Fix with VALUE and TRIM (Stage 1).

questions.question: How do you turn a VLOOKUP #N/A into a friendly label like 'unknown'?
questions.explanation: IFERROR(value, fallback) returns the fallback when the formula errors, keeping your sheet clean.

questions.question: Why teach VLOOKUP as the main lookup, with XLOOKUP only as a note?
options: XLOOKUP is being removed from Excel
VLOOKUP is in legacy files everywhere and works in older Excel; XLOOKUP needs Excel 365/2021+
VLOOKUP is faster on every dataset
questions.explanation: You'll inherit VLOOKUP constantly, and XLOOKUP doesn't exist before Excel 2021. Use XLOOKUP when everyone's on a modern version.
